zh.lifeSummary: William Knapp是Richard Knapp和Agnes Badwell的兒子，出生。他於1499年與Jyllian Jane Knapp在英國英格蘭素福爾可巴爾金結婚。他們至少是10個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1540年之前在英國英格蘭素福爾可。

nameMeanings.name: Knapp
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German: occupational or status name from the German word Knapp(e), a variant of Knabe ‘young unmarried man’. In the 15th century this spelling acquired the separate, specialized meanings ‘servant, apprentice’, and ‘miner’. This surname is also found elsewhere in central Europe, e.g. in Czechia and Slovakia, where it is more commonly spelled Knap (compare 3 below).
German: in Franconia, a nickname for a dexterous or skillful person, of the same ultimate origin as 1 above.
Germanized or Americanized form of Polish, Czech, Slovak, Rusyn, and Slovenian   Knap  , a surname of ultimately German origin (see 1 above).

variants.name: Knappe
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Klaus, Rainer, Siegfried, Wolf.

variants.name: Knap
meanings: Polish, Czech, Slovak, Rusyn, and Slovenian: of German origin (see   Knapp   1), meaning e.g. ‘pageboy, armor bearer’ (Czech), ‘weaver’ (Polish, Slovak, and Rusyn), ‘wool comber’ (Slovak and Slovenian), and ‘miner’ (Slovenian and Czech).

variants.name: Knabe
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Erna, Gerhard, Helmut, Volker.

German: status name for a young man or a page, from Middle High German knabe. In aristocratic circles this term denoted a page or squire (a youth destined to become a knight), while among artisans it referred to a journeyman's assistant or (as a short form of Lehrknabe) ‘apprentice’. In the 15th century a semantic split between Knabe and its variants Knape, Knapp(e) resulted in Knabe meaning ‘boy’ and Knapp(e) ‘servant, apprentice’, and ‘miner’ in modern German.

variants.name: Canup
meanings: Americanized form of North German   Knapp   or   Knopp  .

variants.name: Knupp
meanings: German: variant of   Knapp   or   Knopp  .

variants.name: Knibbs
meanings: English: variant, with post-medieval excrescent -s, of Knibb, which may be related to Knapp or Nobbs or Knipe .

variants.name: Knapper
meanings: English: topographic name from Middle English knap(p)er(e), an agent derivative of knap(pe) ‘knob, hillock’, for someone who lived by a hilltop or hillock; compare Knapp .
German (also Knäpper): habitational name for someone from either of two places in Westphalia named Knapp.
German (Knäpper): unflattering nickname from an agent derivative of knappen ‘to be stingy’ or, in some places, ‘to grab or snatch’.

variants.name: Knapke
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Aloys, Florian.
German: from a pet form of   Knapp  .

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

en.lifeSummary: William Knapp was born as the son of Richard Knapp and Agnes Badwell. He married Jyllian Jane Knapp in 1499, in Barking, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 10 sons and 1 daughter. He died before 1540, in Suffolk, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: William Knapp est né, fils de Richard Knapp et Agnes Badwell. Il a épousé Jyllian Jane Knapp en 1499, à Barking, Suffolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 10 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé avant 1540, à Suffolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: William Knapp wurde, als Sohn von Richard Knapp und Agnes Badwell geboren. Er hat 1499 in Barking, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom Jyllian Jane Knapp geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 10 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er starb vor 1540 in Suffolk, England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: William Knapp è nato il come figlio di Richard Knapp e Agnes Badwell. Lui sposato Jyllian Jane Knapp nel 1499, nel Barking, Suffolk, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 10 figli e 1 figlia. Morì prima del 1540, nel Suffolk, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: William KnappはRichard KnappとAgnes Badwellの息子として生まれた。1499年にイギリスイギリススフォルクバーキングでJyllian Jane Knappと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも10人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は1540年以前にイギリスイギリススフォルクで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: William Knapp(은)는 Richard Knapp(와)과 Agnes Badwell의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 1499년 영국 잉글랜드 수폴크 바킨그에서 Jyllian Jane Knapp(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 열 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1540년 이전 영국 잉글랜드 수폴크에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: William Knapp nasceu filho de Richard Knapp e Agnes Badwell. Ele casou-se com Jyllian Jane Knapp em 1499, em Barking, Suffolk, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 10 filhos e 1 filha. Ele faleceu antes 1540, em Suffolk, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: William Knapp родился. Его родителей звали Richard Knapp и Agnes Badwell. Он женился на женщине по имени Jyllian Jane Knapp в 1499 г. в местности баркинг, суффолк, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 10 сыновей и 1 дочь. Он умер перед 1540 г. в местности суффолк, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: William Knapp nació como hijo de Richard Knapp y Agnes Badwell. Se casó con Jyllian Jane Knapp en 1499, en Barking, Suffolk, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 10 hijos y 1 hija. Murió antes de 1540, en Suffolk, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
